title: Bulk RNA-seq read QC
summary: Assess FASTQ-level quality, adapter burden, and sample readiness before bulk RNA-seq quantification.
test_prompts: Review these bulk RNA-seq FASTQ quality reports and tell me whether any sample should be trimmed or excluded.
Summarize the key pass/fail findings from a multi-sample RNA-seq QC run before quantification.

title: Bulk RNA-seq quantification
summary: Quantify bulk RNA-seq expression with a reproducible path from trimmed reads to gene- or transcript-level abundance.
test_prompts: Choose an appropriate bulk RNA-seq quantification path for paired-end stranded reads and explain the assumptions.
I have trimmed RNA-seq reads and metadata; prepare the expression quantification stage so DE can follow.

title: Bulk RNA-seq count-matrix QC
summary: Inspect count-matrix quality, replicate concordance, and sample-level outliers before differential testing.
test_prompts: Check whether this bulk RNA-seq count matrix is ready for differential expression.
Use sample metadata and counts to identify outliers or metadata mismatches before modeling.

title: Bulk RNA-seq batch correction
summary: Handle batch effects in bulk RNA-seq while preserving the biological factor of interest.
test_prompts: A PCA plot shows batch dominating condition. Explain whether I should adjust in the model or transform the matrix.
Plan a safe batch-correction strategy for bulk RNA-seq with two conditions and one known confounder.

title: Bulk RNA-seq differential expression
summary: Perform contrast-aware differential expression for bulk RNA-seq with explicit design validation and QC-aware reporting.
test_prompts: Run a bulk RNA-seq differential-expression plan for treated versus control with batch in the design.
Check whether this contrast setup is safe before I fit a DE model.

title: Bulk RNA-seq DE visualization
summary: Generate interpretable RNA-seq differential-expression plots that preserve thresholds, directions, and provenance.
test_prompts: Create a plotting plan for bulk RNA-seq differential-expression results without overstating significance.
Summarize which visualizations belong in an RNA-seq DE report and what each one should show.

title: RNA-seq to differential expression workflow
summary: Coordinate the full bulk RNA-seq path from raw reads or quantification outputs to differential-expression results.
test_prompts: Plan an end-to-end bulk RNA-seq workflow from FASTQ files to differential expression.
My RNA-seq project starts from counts instead of FASTQ. Adapt the workflow and keep QC gates explicit.

title: ATAC-seq QC
summary: Review ATAC-seq quality metrics and determine whether a library is suitable for peak-level downstream analysis.
test_prompts: Interpret ATAC-seq QC metrics and decide whether these libraries should proceed to peak calling.
Summarize TSS enrichment, FRiP, and fragment periodicity findings for an ATAC project.

title: ATAC-seq peak calling
summary: Call ATAC-seq peaks with explicit control of genome build, sequencing mode, and ATAC-specific caller parameters.
test_prompts: Plan ATAC-seq peak calling from aligned BAM files and explain the key parameters.
Tell me what you still need before ATAC peak calling if genome build or sequencing mode is unclear.

title: ATAC-seq differential accessibility
summary: Compare chromatin accessibility across groups using a consistent peak universe and replicate-aware modeling.
test_prompts: Design a differential-accessibility analysis after ATAC peak calling with three replicates per group.
Explain how to avoid comparing incompatible peak sets in an ATAC-seq contrast.

title: ATAC-seq motif analysis
summary: Interpret regulatory motif content from ATAC-seq peaks with an explicit background strategy and assay-aware caveats.
test_prompts: Plan motif enrichment on differential ATAC peaks and explain the background requirements.
Summarize what could make ATAC motif results misleading even when peak calling succeeded.

title: ATAC-seq workflow
summary: Coordinate ATAC-seq analysis from QC and peak calling through differential accessibility and motif interpretation.
test_prompts: Outline a complete ATAC-seq workflow with explicit QC gates and downstream branching.
Adapt an ATAC-seq workflow for a project that stops after peak calling instead of differential analysis.

title: ChIP-seq QC
summary: Review ChIP-seq assay quality, control adequacy, and replicate consistency before peak interpretation.
test_prompts: Interpret these ChIP-seq QC summaries and tell me whether peak calling should proceed.
Explain what QC metrics matter most for histone marks versus transcription-factor ChIP.

title: ChIP-seq peak calling
summary: Call ChIP-seq peaks with explicit control handling and mark-aware narrow or broad parameter choices.
test_prompts: Plan ChIP-seq peak calling for H3K27me3 and explain what changes compared with TF ChIP.
Tell me what you need from the user before ChIP peak calling if input controls are missing.

title: ChIP-seq differential binding
summary: Compare ChIP-seq occupancy across groups using a stable region universe and replicate-aware contrasts.
test_prompts: Design a differential-binding analysis for ChIP-seq with two conditions and biological replicates.
Explain how you would construct a stable binding matrix before statistical testing.

title: ChIP-seq peak annotation
summary: Annotate ChIP-seq peaks against genes and genomic features while keeping coordinate and build provenance explicit.
test_prompts: Annotate ChIP-seq peaks to nearby genes and explain how genome-build mismatches would break the result.
Tell me which annotation choices materially change the biological interpretation of ChIP peaks.

title: ChIP-seq workflow
summary: Coordinate ChIP-seq analysis from assay QC and peak calling through annotation and differential binding.
test_prompts: Plan a ChIP-seq workflow for a transcription-factor study with input controls and replicates.
Adapt the ChIP workflow for a broad histone mark and keep the stage gates explicit.

title: Small-variant calling
summary: Prepare and assess germline or somatic small-variant calling with explicit caller assumptions and QC gates.
test_prompts: Outline a small-variant calling strategy from aligned reads and explain what assumptions must be fixed first.
Tell me how germline and somatic variant-calling plans diverge before filtering.

title: Variant filtering
summary: Filter small variants with explicit thresholds, provenance, and protection against over-aggressive exclusion.
test_prompts: Design a safe variant-filtering plan and explain how each threshold changes sensitivity versus specificity.
I need to filter a VCF for downstream clinical review without hiding plausible candidates.

title: Variant annotation
summary: Annotate variants with consequence, frequency, pathogenicity, and evidence-source provenance.
test_prompts: Annotate a filtered variant set and explain which evidence layers matter most for prioritization.
Show how transcript choice can materially change a variant annotation result.

title: Variant clinical interpretation
summary: Interpret annotated variants for reporting with explicit evidence boundaries and audience-aware caveats.
test_prompts: Interpret a candidate variant for a report and spell out the evidence and uncertainty clearly.
Explain why a clinical variant interpretation should not be written until the reporting framework is fixed.

title: Structural-variant calling
summary: Assess structural-variant detection with explicit breakpoint support, SV class expectations, and QC limits.
test_prompts: Plan structural-variant calling and tell me which support metrics you would inspect before trusting a call.
Explain how SV detection changes between short-read and long-read settings.

title: FASTQ to variants workflow
summary: Coordinate the variant-analysis path from raw sequencing data through calls, filters, annotation, and handoff-ready results.
test_prompts: Plan an end-to-end variant workflow from sequencing reads to an analysis-ready annotated VCF.
Adapt the workflow when the project needs both small variants and structural variants.

title: Single-cell preprocessing
summary: Preprocess single-cell data with explicit thresholds for cells, features, mitochondrial burden, and doublets.
test_prompts: Outline a single-cell preprocessing plan and explain which thresholds should be justified rather than copied blindly.
Review a single-cell QC summary and decide whether filtering thresholds are too aggressive.

title: Single-cell batch integration
summary: Integrate single-cell datasets while preserving biological structure and avoiding over-correction.
test_prompts: Choose an integration strategy for multi-batch single-cell RNA-seq and explain the over-correction risk.
Tell me how you would verify that single-cell batch integration preserved the biology of interest.

title: Single-cell clustering
summary: Cluster single-cell data with interpretable resolution choices and cluster-stability checks.
test_prompts: Plan clustering for an integrated single-cell dataset and explain how you would choose resolution.
Summarize what cluster diagnostics should be reviewed before labeling cell states.

title: Single-cell cell annotation
summary: Assign cell identities with explicit evidence, uncertainty, and reference-awareness.
test_prompts: Explain how you would annotate clusters in a single-cell dataset without overstating confidence.
I have marker genes and a reference atlas. Plan a cell-annotation workflow that keeps ambiguity visible.

title: Single-cell differential expression
summary: Perform single-cell differential testing with attention to donor structure, pseudo-bulk options, and cluster context.
test_prompts: Plan single-cell differential expression between conditions while respecting donor structure.
Explain when pseudo-bulk is preferable to cell-level DE for single-cell RNA-seq.

title: Single-cell trajectory inference
summary: Infer single-cell trajectories only when the data support a biologically defensible progression structure.
test_prompts: Assess whether this single-cell project is suitable for trajectory inference before suggesting pseudotime.
Describe a careful trajectory-analysis path for a developmental single-cell dataset.

title: Single-cell RNA-seq workflow
summary: Coordinate single-cell RNA-seq analysis from preprocessing through integration, clustering, annotation, and downstream interpretation.
test_prompts: Plan a complete single-cell RNA-seq workflow and highlight where QC should stop the pipeline.
Adapt the workflow when the goal is atlas building rather than condition-level DE.

title: Methylation alignment
summary: Prepare bisulfite-aware alignment outputs that are valid inputs for methylation extraction and DMR analysis.
test_prompts: Plan methylation alignment from bisulfite reads and explain which protocol details change the setup.
Tell me what alignment QC you need before calling methylation.

title: Methylation calling
summary: Extract and summarize methylation calls with explicit coverage thresholds and protocol-aware caveats.
test_prompts: Plan methylation call extraction after bisulfite alignment and explain what the main outputs mean.
Summarize the assumptions behind per-CpG methylation calling for downstream DMR work.

title: Methylation QC
summary: Review methylation-specific QC such as conversion efficiency, M-bias, and coverage before downstream comparisons.
test_prompts: Interpret methylation QC outputs and tell me whether the sample should proceed to DMR analysis.
Explain how M-bias and conversion efficiency affect trust in methylation results.

title: Differential methylation region detection
summary: Detect differential methylation regions with explicit replicate handling, coverage thresholds, and region-level reporting.
test_prompts: Design a DMR analysis for two groups with biological replicates and uneven coverage.
Explain when methylation coverage is too sparse to trust DMR results.

title: Methylation workflow
summary: Coordinate methylation analysis from alignment and call extraction through QC and DMR detection.
test_prompts: Plan a methylation workflow from bisulfite reads to DMR detection with explicit QC gates.
Adapt a methylation workflow when the user only has methylation call tables instead of raw reads.

title: GO enrichment
summary: Run GO over-representation analysis with explicit background-universe and identifier-handling decisions.
test_prompts: Plan GO enrichment for a thresholded DEG set and tell me what background universe you need.
Explain why identifier mapping can distort GO enrichment results.

title: GSEA
summary: Perform ranked-list enrichment with explicit metric choice, directional interpretation, and leading-edge handling.
test_prompts: Explain how you would set up GSEA from a full differential-expression ranking.
Tell me what can go wrong if a user gives you only a short thresholded list and asks for GSEA.

title: Reactome enrichment
summary: Run Reactome-focused enrichment while preserving pathway hierarchy and mechanistic context.
test_prompts: Plan Reactome enrichment from differential-expression results and explain how you would handle redundant pathways.
Summarize why Reactome results need hierarchy-aware interpretation instead of flat ranking.

title: Pathway visualization
summary: Turn enrichment outputs into pathway figures that preserve direction, magnitude, and evidence context.
test_prompts: Design a pathway-visualization set for an RNA-seq project that includes ORA and GSEA results.
Explain how to visualize enrichment without hiding thresholds or directionality.

title: Expression to pathways workflow
summary: Coordinate the handoff from expression or region-level differential results into enrichment, visualization, and biological interpretation.
test_prompts: Plan a workflow that starts from differential-expression results and ends in pathway interpretation.
Adapt the workflow when the user has ranked genes but no thresholded DEG list.

title: Variant to report workflow
summary: Coordinate variant filtering, annotation, and reporting-oriented interpretation into a traceable reporting workflow.
test_prompts: Plan a workflow from filtered variants to a reporting-ready interpretation package.
Explain how the reporting workflow should change when the audience is research-only rather than clinical.

title: Bioinformatics orchestrator
summary: Orchestrate multi-stage bioinformatics analysis across canonical skills while preserving handoff artifacts and QC gates.
test_prompts: Route a complex project that spans RNA-seq differential expression and pathway interpretation through canonical skills.
Break a multi-assay request into safe sub-skills and define the QC gates between them.
